Introduction.p1: Hello everyone, thank you for reading my post. My name is <bold>Nguyen Ha Vy</bold>.
Introduction.p2: I graduated from a university of Economics in 2025. At the time of writing this, it is 2026, and I am working an office job unrelated to the IT field. My daily tasks mainly involve data entry, compiling data from Excel, and processing documents.
Introduction.p3: However, after working for a while, I realized that my current job does not offer many long-term growth opportunities. Additionally, the company has been trending toward downsizing, which made me start thinking more seriously about a new career path.
Introduction.p4: After doing some research, I decided to pursue a career as a <bold>Tester</bold> in the IT field. However, I must admit that my starting point is quite low:
Introduction.li2: I don't know anyone in the industry who could give me advice or guidance.
Introduction.li3: And at this point, I have virtually no knowledge about IT in general and Testing in particular.
Introduction.p5: Previously, I posted a few articles in the <link><bold>Testing VN</bold></link> group on Facebook to ask for advice. I received both encouragement and practical, thoughtful feedback from people in the industry. Those opinions left me somewhat uncertain, but they also helped me understand that the road ahead would not be easy.
Introduction.p6: Right now, what I can do is start with my own effort. Due to limited finances, I cannot yet enroll in paid courses, so my plan is to self-study in the evenings and on weekends to gradually learn and build a foundation of knowledge in Testing.
Introduction.p7: I wrote this post hoping to receive more advice, shared experiences, or learning guidance from those who are currently working in the industry. Any input would be incredibly valuable to me at this point.
Introduction.p8: I created this site to record my entire learning journey and career transition into Testing, like a study journal to track my progress over time.
Introduction.p9: Thank you everyone for taking the time to read this 🥰.

0.title: Become a Tester in 6 Months
0.description: A complete self-study roadmap for transitioning into a QA/Tester role from scratch — covering fundamentals, manual testing, API testing, automation, and job preparation.

1.title: CSS Fundamentals
1.description: Learn how to style web pages using selectors, the box model, flexbox, grid, and responsive design principles.

2.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
2.description: Learn the core concepts of JavaScript — variables, functions, DOM manipulation, events, and async programming.

3.title: HTML Fundamentals
3.description: Learn the building blocks of the web — document structure, semantic elements, forms, and accessibility basics.

TesterPlanPage.title: Become a Tester in 6 Months
TesterPlanPage.overview: A self-study roadmap for transitioning into a QA/Tester role from scratch. No IT background, no budget for paid courses — just consistent effort in evenings and weekends.

phase1.title: Software Testing Fundamentals
phase1.goal: Understand what testing is, its role in software development, and essential QA concepts.

phase2.title: Manual Testing & Test Case Design
phase2.goal: Write effective test cases and test plans, and apply black-box and white-box testing techniques.

phase3.title: API Testing with Postman
phase3.goal: Get hands-on with REST APIs: sending requests, writing assertions, and organizing test collections.

phase4.title: Automation Testing Basics
phase4.goal: Learn to write basic automated UI tests using free, widely-used tools.

phase5.title: SQL & Database Testing
phase5.goal: Understand how to validate data at the database level using basic SQL queries.

phase6.title: Job Preparation
phase6.goal: Build a portfolio, prepare a CV, and get ready for junior Tester interviews.

HtmlPlanPage.title: HTML Fundamentals
HtmlPlanPage.overview: A 5-day plan to learn the core building blocks of the web. Covers document structure, semantic HTML, forms, media, and accessibility basics.

day1.title: Document Structure & Core Tags
day1.goal: Understand how an HTML document is structured and learn the most common tags.

day2.title: Semantic HTML
day2.goal: Learn to write meaningful HTML using semantic elements that improve readability and SEO.

day3.title: Forms & Input Types
day3.goal: Build interactive forms using various input types and understand form submission basics.

day4.title: Tables, Media & Embedding
day4.goal: Display structured data with tables and embed media such as images, video, and audio.

day5.title: Accessibility & Best Practices
day5.goal: Write accessible, clean HTML that works well for all users and assistive technologies.

CssPlanPage.title: CSS Fundamentals
CssPlanPage.overview: A 4-day plan to learn how to style web pages. Covers the box model, flexbox, grid, responsive design, and basic animations.

day1.title: Selectors, Specificity & Box Model
day1.goal: Understand how CSS rules are applied and how the box model controls layout and spacing.

day2.title: Flexbox
day2.goal: Use flexbox to build one-dimensional layouts with flexible, responsive behavior.

day3.title: Grid & Responsive Design
day3.goal: Create two-dimensional layouts with CSS Grid and make pages adapt to different screen sizes.

day4.title: Transitions, Animations & CSS Variables
day4.goal: Add motion and dynamic theming to pages using CSS transitions, keyframe animations, and custom properties.

JsPlanPage.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
JsPlanPage.overview: A 1-week plan to learn the core concepts of JavaScript. Covers variables, functions, DOM manipulation, events, and async programming.

day1.title: Variables, Data Types & Operators
day1.goal: Understand the basic building blocks of JavaScript: how to store and work with data.

day2.title: Control Flow & Functions
day2.goal: Control program logic with conditionals and loops, and organize code into reusable functions.

day3.title: Arrays & Objects
day3.goal: Work with collections of data using arrays and objects, including common built-in methods.

day4.title: DOM Manipulation
day4.goal: Read and modify HTML elements and styles directly from JavaScript.

day5.title: Events
day5.goal: Respond to user interactions like clicks, inputs, and keyboard actions.

day6.title: Async Programming
day6.goal: Understand asynchronous JavaScript and how to fetch data from APIs.

day7.title: ES6+ Features & Practice
day7.goal: Review modern JavaScript syntax and apply everything learned in a small hands-on project.

Testimonials.title: Community Feedback
Testimonials.subtitle: Advice and encouragement I received from people in the Testing community.

0.name: Minh Tuan
0.comment: You seem to have the right mindset for a Tester — careful, detail-oriented, and curious enough to ask questions. Most importantly, you're taking action instead of just thinking about it. Keep going. The first step is always the hardest.

1.name: Lan Anh
1.comment: I started from zero too, just like you. Focus on understanding the testing process, writing clear bug reports, and practicing as much as you can. Don't give up when it gets hard — that's when the real learning happens.

2.name: Hoang Phuc
2.role: Junior Tester · 1 year in the industry
2.comment: I passed my first Tester interview after 4 months of self-study with no IT background. A learning journal like this is great — it helps you review what you've learned and shows recruiters that you're serious. Keep it up!
